data.title: The Alkaline Water Company signs letter of intent for equipment acquisition
data.description:
• The Alkaline Water Company (OTC:WTER) announced the signing of a letter of intent with FZA Note Buyers, a Florida limited liability company, for a strategic equipment acquisition proposed to be fulfilled through the issuance of 34M shares of common stock subject to a 12-month lock-up period.
  • Following closing, the Company plans a rapid 30-day implementation timeline for equipment integration into its production infrastructure.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Sadot Group appoints Catia Jorge as CEO
data.description:
• Sadot Group (NASDAQ:SDOT) on Thursday announced the appointment of Catia Jorge as its new chief executive officer, effective immediately.
  • Jorge, who succeeds Michael Roper, most recently served as Brazil Country Head and Vice President/Grains Business Head Latin America for Olam Agri. Prior to Olam, she held leadership roles at Cargill Agricola South America.
  • Roper will transition as chief governance and compliance officer, Sadot said, adding that he will also continue to lead the initiative to divest the company's remaining restaurant operations.
  • SDOT +4.34% premarket to $2.88.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Edesa Biotech shares jump on $15M private placement
data.description:
• Edesa Biotech (NASDAQ:EDSA) shares rose more than 70% in Thursday pre-market trading after it said that it has sold, in a private placement, an aggregate of 834 newly designated Series B-1 convertible preferred shares and 3.5M common shares in a private placement priced at-the-market.
  • The purchase price per preferred share was $10,000 and the purchase price per common share was $1.92. Officers and directors of the company purchased approximately $1.1 million of the securities sold in the offering.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering were approximately $15M before deducting any offering-related expenses.
  • Edesa currently expects to use the net proceeds from the offering, to fund the continued advancement of EB06, its CXCL10 monoclonal antibody, into a Phase 2 clinical study in subjects with nonsegmental vitiligo, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: KULR and Worksport partner to advance battery technology and U.S. manufacturing
data.description:
• KULR Technology (NYSE:KULR) and Worksport (WKSP) announced, on Thursday, a strategic partnership focused on advancing battery technology and strengthening domestic manufacturing.
  • The collaboration centers on three key areas: joint battery pack development, thermal runaway protection, and AI-integrated battery management system software design.
  • KULR will utilize its KULR ONE Design Solutions to evaluate the performance, cost efficiency, and scalability of integrated battery solutions for the Worksport COR system.
  • As demand for U.S.-based production increases, KULR and Worksport are exploring domestic manufacturing options for the COR Battery Pack and related products.
  • Through the integration of its thermal management technology and AI-powered BMS into Worksport’s products, KULR expects to open new revenue streams.
  • KULR Technology (KULR) shares were up more than 1.3% in pre-market trading, while Worksport (WKSP) shares were up more than 4%.

data.title: Deere’s stock declines on grim outlook for farm machinery
data.description:
Deere’s (NYSE:DE) stock fell 6% in premarket trading Thursday after the maker of farming and construction equipment reported a decline in earnings and forecast a significant drop in sales for the year.

Falling crop prices has led to weak demand for the company’s products. Deere (NYSE:DE) last year said it would cut jobs to reduce expenses.

For its fiscal first quarter ended January 26, Deere’s (DE) net income was $869 million, or $3.19 a share, down from $1.751 billion, or $6.23 a share, a year earlier.

Sales fell 30% from a year earlier to $8.508 billion, beating the average estimate of $7.89 billion among Wall Street analysts surveyed by S&P Global Intelligence.

“Deere’s (DE) performance in the first quarter highlights our continued focus on optimizing inventory levels of both new and used equipment amidst the uncertain market conditions our customers are facing,” John May, chief executive of the company, said in prepared remarks.

Guidance for 2025

Management expects fiscal 2025 net income in the range of $5.0 billion to $5.5 billion, compared with the consensus estimate of $5.3 billion.

Sales at its production and precision agriculture unit are expected to decline 15% to 20% for the full year. The company estimated small agriculture and turf sales to decline by 10% and construction and forestry sales to drop 10% to 15%.

The guidance doesn’t include possible effects of tariffs on imports and trade disputes between the U.S. and other countries.

Fiscal first quarter results

Deere’s production and precision agriculture sales slumped by 37% in the first quarter to $3.067 billion, below the $3.525 billion consensus. Slower shipment volumes led to the decline as its operating profit fell 68% to $338 million.

Small agriculture and turf sales dropped 28% to $1.748 billion to miss the $1.960 billion consensus.

Construction and forestry sales fell 38% to $1.994 billion. Financial services sales rose 11% to $230 million.

data.title: LightPath Technologies acquires G5 infrared
data.description:
• LightPath Technologies (NASDAQ:LPTH) on Thursday announced the accretive acquisition of G5 Infrared, an infrared camera systems manufacturer, for a combination of cash and stock.
  • The company said the aggregate consideration payable to the G5 members is $27 million, consisting of $20.25 million in cash and $6.75 million in common shares.
  • "This accretive acquisition strengthens our financial profile, creating a combined company that we believe will achieve revenue of at least $55 million in the twelve months following the acquisition and increased EBITDA margins," it added.
  • LightPath Technologies has alos entered into definitive agreements to finance the cash consideration payable to the G5 members through private placements of $25.5 million of a newly-created series of 6.5% fixed price convertible preferred equity and related warrants, common shares totaling $1.5 million, and senior secured promissory notes totaling $5.2 million.
  • LPTH -2.56% premarket to $3.42.
  • Source: Press Release
